description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.
The linux_networking package
sdsdddsdsds

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.
A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.

readme: movie_publisher package tools movie files ROS (playback, conversion bag files handles file formats system installation ffmpeg decode. Important: package meant work moviepy. due packaging issues, moviepy installed automatically dependency. fallback OpenCV, worse. install moviepy manually calling: sudo pip install moviepy rosdep install python-moviepy-pip Main tools movie_publisher_node: ROS node serves video file video topic source (sensor_msgs/Image friends). movie_publisher.launch: launch file convenient usage node. starting image_transport/republish node converts video stream raw compressed/theora. mode, convert video files bagfiles batch. movie_to_bag: batch script takes video input transforms bag file. add_movie_to_bag: batch script adds video topic existing bag file. Helper tools fix_bag_timestamps: batch script rewrites bag files message publication time message header. generate bagfile data high speed reprocess suitable publication timestamps. merge.py: copy srv_tools/merge.py indigo. movie_publisher_node node run backends moviepy opencv. moviepy strongly recommended, ffmpeg, versatile efficient. set backend param, autodetection run. Published topics movie (sensor_msgs/Image): published movie, raw format. Node-private parameters: movie_file (string, required): Path movie play. format ffmpeg decode. fps (float, optional): set, playback FPS (speeding up/slowing movie). start (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback start time. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set duration. (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback stop time affected start). expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start duration. duration (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback duration. set, duration counted clip, duration start clip. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start loop (bool, default False): loop movie node shut Exludes (bool, default False): True, movie processed published quickly waiting real time. timestamps resulting messages act “real-world-like” FPS means frames’ timestamps sec set fake_time_start timestamps non-zero time. Excludes loop. playback_rate (float, optional): set number, mode play fast rate (set rate number lose messages, image_transport/republish). fake_time_start (float, default timestamp message. frame_id (string, default frame_id messages’ headers. spin_after_end (bool, default False): True, rospy.spin() called movie published. verbose (bool, default False): True, logs info frame played. wait_after_publisher_created (float, default workaround case give subscribers time publisher created. Tweak number missing start messages. publisher_queue_size (int, default mode, queue_size movie publisher. backend (string, default “moviepy”): backend reading video. moviepy opencv. moviepy selected opencv support codecs). ffmpeg (string, default nonempty, specifies (absolute) path ffmpeg binary movie_publisher.launch launch file takes arguments node’s parameters. Additionally, takes arguments: transport (string, default 'raw'): Type image transport. launch file start image_transport/republish node publishes video encoded transport type. republished_topic_basename (string, default movie_$(arg transport)): Base topic serve republished messages. Full topic $(arg republished_topic_basename)/$(arg transport) $(arg republished_topic_basename) case raw transport). base topic movie_publisher_node subscribes (movie default). movie_to_bag Convert movie file bag file video topic. description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional jBrgment </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface

description: UWSim is an UnderWater SIMulator for marine robotics research and development. UWSim visualizes an underwater virtual scenario that can be configured using standard modeling software. Controllable underwater vehicles, surface vessels and robotic manipulators, as well as simulated sensors, can be added to the scene and accessed externally through ROS interfaces. This allows to easily integrate the visualization tool with existing control architectures.

description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current

     priority and timeout are configured through a YAML file, that can be reload at runtime.

description: The geonav_transform package
readme: geonav_transform goal package simplify integration accurate/precise geographic navigation information (typically sensor) ROS localization navigation workflows. ends, geonav_tranform_node perform functions: Takes incoming Odometry messages, typically sensor, geographic position sensor-frame orientation velocities. (Note, nice message specifies type input Odometry bit hack.) Tranforms message Odometry message express information frames utm odom Broadcasts tranforms utm->odom odom->base_link use-case motivated project integrating sensors provide GPS-aided INS solution microstrain, advanced navigation, Xsens, situation analogous ekf/ukf node robot_localization package fuse IMU GPS information, case processing sensor. purpose package integration type sensor ROS navigation stack. Parameters ~datum: origin local “odom” frame element array [Latitude, Logitude, Altitude]. Lat/Lon decimal degrees; altitude meters. Default ~frequency: frequency broadcasting tranforms. Odometry messages published rate incoming Odometry messages. Default ~broadcast_utm2odom_transform: broadcast utm->odom tranform. Default True. ~broadcast_odom2base_transform: broadcast odom->base_link tranform. Default True. ~zero_altitude ~base_link_frame_id: Default “base_link” ~odom_frame_id: Default “odom” ~utm_frame_id: Default “utm” Subscribed Topics /odometry/nav: nav_msgs/Odometry message geographic position velocity data. message organized header.frame_id child_frame_id values pose.pose.position Latitude [dec. degrees] Longitude [dec. degrees] Altitude pose.pose.orientation base_link relative fixed ENU coordinate frame ~orientation_ned parameter set true, node convert orientation NED ENU. assuming orientation true magnetic). Typically magnetic declination set internal sensor providing information. pose.covariance expressed meters position radians orientation (REP-103) twist.twist.linear/angular velocity base_link frame twist.covariance expressed rad/s. Published Topics message published rate incoming /odometry/nav messages. Consistent navigation stack robot_localization, Odometry messages position pose information relative fixed frame (utm odom, reported header.frame_id). velocity (twist) information relative mobile frame (base_link, reported .child_frame). /odometry/odom: nav_msgs/Odometry message local odom frame (relative datum) /odometry/utm: nav_msgs/Odometry message UTM frame published static tranform: http://wiki.ros.org/tf2_ros http://wiki.ros.org/tf2/Tutorials/Writing%20a%20tf2%20static%20broadcaster%20(C%2B%2B) Published Transforms utm->odom odom->base_link Frames utm: global UTM coordinate frame. origin frame UTM zone determined datum parameter odom: local, fixed odom frame orgin datum parameter. assumed orientation UTM odom frame. general simplifies implementation, usage interpretation. base_link: mobile frame typically coincides sensor frame.

hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

The hector_worldmodel stack helps to collect and fuse information about objects in the world.
    It is used by Team Hector Darmstadt to localize victims and QR codes in the Robocup Rescue
    scenario. See

hector_worldmodel_geotiff_plugins contains a plugin to visualize worldmodel objects (e.g. victims in a

hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

This is a programming framework to facilitate application development involving robot swarms. It makes coding for swarms much easier by providing an adequate swarm-level abstraction, as well as tools for swarm management, various communication mechanisms and so on. It also provides essential data structures, such as Neighbor, Swarm, and Virtual Stigmergy, to the user. Most importantly, it is completely compatible with ROS Indigo and presented in the form of a C++ library, which means that all resources in the ROS ecosystem are still available to the user. It is currently  extensible to Opensplice DDS.
authors: Xuefeng Chang, Zhongxuan Cai, Yanzhen Wang, Xiaodong Yi

description: Implementation of the MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP)
readme: MultiWii Cleanflight Baseflight Communication Library library implements MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP) communicating MultiWii Cleanflight flight controller serial device. defines low-level API sending+encoding receiving+decoding MSP messages, high-level API subscriber pattern periodically request data call callback functions message received. communication tested MultiWii Arduino Nano achieve update rate approximately cycle time Betaflight Naze32 Rev6 update rates max. Installation Test Linux (Ubuntu Debian) install asio ninja: sudo apt install --no-install-recommends ninja-build libasio-dev check source code cmake compile: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake --build build run program path serial device: ./msp_read_test /dev/ttyUSB0 Windows Requirements CMake asio: download asio-1.20.0.zip extract header files, C:\asio-1.20.0 Visual Build Tools: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/de/downloads/ Build Test open Developer Command Prompt Visual Studio change directory checked msp run: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DASIO_ROOT=C:\asio-1.20.0 cmake --build build test serial device: msp_read_test.exe Hardware Setup connect Arduino Naze32 boards built-in USB-to-Serial connector direct serial connection (RX->TX, TX->RX, GND->GND, VCC->VCC). option preferable high transfer rates, direct connection exposed transmission errors. MultiWii MSP update rate determined variable LOOP_TIME config.h #define LOOP_TIME sets loop time update rate Arduino boards integrated USB-to-serial converter auto-reset serial connection established. send messages opening connection request send response. prevent delay opening connection sending messages, disabling Data Terminal Ready (DTR) line, stty /dev/ttyUSB0 -hupcl. Cleanflight Betaflight change update rate serial task range set serial_update_rate_hz=2000, save increase serial baudrate Ports configuration tab Sending Receiving commands activate feature RX_MSP GUI: Configuration Receiver Receiver Mode MSP input CLI: execute feature RX_MSP Beginning Cleanflight Betaflight MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets insufficient flash memory naze32). verify MSP_RC messages return empty list channels. activate MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets, add #define USE_RX_MSP target.h, src/main/target/NAZE/target.h. FlightContoller API FlightContoller API user send/receive messages flight controller. Messages queried periodically, demand. Instantiation Instantiate setup FlightController class: #include <FlightController.hpp> fcu::FlightController fcu; connection setup fcu.connect("/dev/ttyUSB0", Periodic request messages Messages queried periodically handled automatically subscription. class method stand-alone function. description: myahrs_driver is a driver package for the WITHROBOT's myAHRS+. The myAHRS+ is a low cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) with USB/UART/I2C interface. The myAHRS+ board contains a 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, a 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer and a 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. The driver should also work with USB port.
readme: myahrs_driver Overview driver package WITHROBOT’s myAHRS+ http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs lilliputdirect http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 myAHRS+ cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) USB/UART/I2C interface. myAHRS+ board 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. driver work USB port. Axes Convention myAHRS+ board NED type. myahrs_driver contained package converts frame conventions ROS east north (ENU) convention hand rule) publishing msgs. driver coordinate frame http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#axis-orientation information. left NED type IMU: x-north, y-east, z-down, relative magnetic north. ENU type IMU: x-east, y-north, z-up, relative magnetic north. Original Source original source support ROS) maintained github tutorials wiki page. visualization test 3D-box included original source. package myAHRS+ SDK https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus Video visualization demonstration RViz. Forum myAHRS+ user here(http://forum.odroid.com/viewforum.php?f=109). Bindings between openrave's controller interface and ROS's action lib interface for controlling robots.

Package uses openrave planning and the robot_calibration stacks to calibrate a robot's sensors. The calibration only depends on the openrave/collada descriptions, therefore it is meant to be a relatively simple way of calibrating new robots with one-click.

    Maintained by the JSK lab at University of Tokyo.

    - librobot_control - openrave plugin for ros client to talk to robot services
    - openravecontroller.h - base class for robot server using services
    - tfsender - given joint positions of the robot, sends the correct tf frames

    For more information on how to use this inside the openrave framework, see:

cameras webcams Freenect: Kinect Pionner_dashboard: GUI dashboard window Usage starting robot default parameters: roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch activate features arguments: Argument values Default Description controller rosaria, p2os rosaria Hardware driver controller controller_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyS0 Serial port communicate controller sonar true, false false Activate sonars laser hokuyo, lms_1xx, lms_2xx, lms_1xxx, lms_4xxx, lms_5xx, mrs_1xxx, mrs_6xxx, rms_3xx, tim_5xx, tim_7xx, tim_7xxS false Laser reference code ‘false’ laser usbcam true, false false launch usbcam node kinect true, false false launch kinect nodes dashboard true, false false launch GUI window rqt visualize sensors readings control motors roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch controller:=p2os laser:=lms_5xx usbcam:=true controls Laser node: arguments lidar parameters: Argument values Default Description laser_hostname Address Laser address hostname applicable) laser_serial_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyUSB0 Laser Port applicable) laser_baselink_distance roll pitch yaw Distance baselink lidar meters setting broadcast) roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch laser:=lms_1xx laser_hostname:=192.168.0.4 laser_baselink_distance:='0.20 controls Camera node: arguments customize camera parameters: Argument Values Default Description camera_port /dev/videox device number) /dev/video0 USB Camera port camera_display true, false true Display video feed screen stop parameters: customize parameters config/camera.yaml file, image_width, image_height, pixel_format, camera_frame_id io_method. roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch usbcam:=true camera_display:=false camera_port:=/dev/video2 controls Kinect node: arguments customize kinect parameters: Argument Values Default Description kinect_data_skip Number frames skip kinect data kinect_id kinect device number roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch kinect:=true kinect_data_skip:=5 issues open /dev/ttyUSB0: Permission denied frequent issue USB-Serial adapter connect robot hardware lidar. description: Messages, serivices and actions for SawYer roch
Metapackage for SawYer roch robot software

A controller ensuring the safe operation of roch.

    The SafetyController keeps track of ult, psd and cliff events. In case of the first three,
    roch is commanded to move back. 
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (cliff, psd and ult etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!
    Refrence with yujinrobot' kobuki.

Ult/psd/cliff to pointcloud:
    Publish Ult, cliff, and psd sensors events as points in a pointcloud, so navistack can use them
    for poor-man navigation. Implemented intended to run together with roch_base.

User notes default, websocket URL determined host webpage served localhost, robot.university.edu). scroll horizontally view names cut holding Shift scrolling. Install Node.js nvm don’t Install nvm nvm install node Install Polymer CLI don’t npm install polymer-cli Download frontend dependencies: frontend bower update Run local development server: polymer serve Visit http://localhost:8081 web browser. description: Converts between Python dictionaries and JSON to rospy messages.
readme: rospy_message_converter Rospy_message_converter lightweight ROS package Python library convert Python dictionaries JSON messages rospy messages, vice versa. ROS ROS branches ROS users master branch. ROS users branch distro (foxy/galactic/humble/rolling/…). Usage Convert dictionary ROS message rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String dictionary 'data': 'Howdy' message message_converter.convert_dictionary_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', dictionary) Convert ROS message dictionary rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Howdy') dictionary message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_dictionary(message) Convert JSON ROS message rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String json_str '{"data": "Hello"}' message json_message_converter.convert_json_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', json_str) Convert ROS message JSON rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Hello') json_str json_message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_json(message) Test run tests: catkin_make test pre-commit Formatting Checks repo pre-commit check runs locally set run automatically commit install, pip: pip3 install --user pre-commit run files repo manually: pre-commit run run pre-commit automatically committing local repo, install git hooks: pre-commit install License Project released BSD license. Edit config/parameters.yaml file based ROS visualization tool rqt_plot PlotJuggler, visualize statistics interested Move nodes machines, observe setup effective, based statistics PlotJuggler specific setup order PlotJuggler work intended, follow simple steps: Run PlotJuggler. Navigate Streaming Start: ROS Topic Streamer. popup dialog, tick tickboxes, click “Edit Rules” button. Open plotjuggler.rules.xml file paste contents </SubstitutionRules> tag dialog popped clicked “Edit Rules”. Select “Confirm Close”. description: A wrapper for keeping rqt programs alive.
readme: RQT Wrapper Overview Wrap rqt plugin automagically ROS master solves frustration restart rqt applications laptop/pc time teardown robot relaunch Incidentally save time effort WRQT Reconfigure ROS Core WRQT Reconfigure Absent ROS Core source code released BSD 3-Clause license. Status Beta. Areas addressed: Desktop notifications dialog ros Reflect arguments rqt programs handle, Usage lines. wraps rqt_reconfigure plugin: import rqt_wrapper import sys '__main__': rqt_wrapper rqt_wrapper.RQTWrapper('rqt_reconfigure.param_plugin.ParamPlugin') sys.exit(rqt_wrapper.exec_()) Programs common rqt programs recreated wrqt_xxx prefix deposited global bin directory. wrqt_graph wrqt_reconfigure wrqt_robot_monitor

description: A ROS package that implements a multi-robot RRT-based map exploration algorithm. It also has the image-based frontier detection that uses image processing to extract frontier points
readme: rrt_exploration ROS package implements multi-robot map exploration algorithm mobile robots. based Rapidly-Exploring Random Tree (RRT) algorithm. occupancy girds map representation.The packgae ROS nodes: Global RRT frontier point detector node. Local RRT frontier point detector node. Filter node. Assigner node. opencv-based frontier detector node. Youtube playlist showing package running single/multiple robots, real setup (Kobuki robots) simulation (Gazebo). Note: package written master’s thesis American University Sharjah. thesis advisor Shayok Mukhopadhyay. package research work, cite papers: Multi-robot Map Exploration Based Multiple Rapidly-Exploring Randomized Trees. Autonomous robotic exploration based multiple rapidly-exploring randomized trees. Requirements package tested ROS Kinetic ROS Indigo, work distributions Jade. requirements needed installing package: installed ROS distribution (indigo Recommended indigo kinetic). Created workspace. Installed “gmapping” ROS package: Ubuntu, running ROS Kinectic, typing command terminal: sudo apt-get install ros-kinetic-gmapping Install ROS navigation stack. command (assuming Ubuntu, ROS Kinetic): sudo apt-get install ros-kinetic-navigation Python tested Python have/install python modules: -OpenCV sudo apt-get install python-opencv -Numpy sudo apt-get install python-numpy -Sklearn sudo apt-get install python-scikits-learn Installation Download package place folder workspace. compile ```catkin_make Setting Robots package exploration strategy single multiple robots. work, set robots ready [navigation stack](http://wiki.ros.org/navigation). Additionally, robots set prepared Note: quickly run test package, [rrt_exploration_tutorials](https://github.com/hasauino/rrt_exploration_tutorials) package Gazebo simulation single multiple robots, package. Robots Network multi-robot configuration, package require special network configuration, simply works single ROS master robots). robots, ```ROS_MASTER_URI parameter pointing master's address. information setting ROS multiple machines, follow [this](http://wiki.ros.org/ROS/NetworkSetup) link. Robot's frame names robot’s frames prefixed Naming robots starts “/robot_1”, “/robot_2”, “/robot_3”, package single robot, robot’s frames prefixed /robot_1). robot_1, frames tree Robot’s node topic names nodes topics running robot prefixed robot node names /robot_1/move_base_node /robot_1/slam_gmapping topic names /robot_1/odom /robot_1/map /robot_1/base_scan Setting navigation stack robots move_base_node node, brings navigation stack robot, running. package (rrt_exploration) generates target exploration goals, robot able receive points move navigation stack needed. Additionally, robot global local cost maps. proivded move_base_node mapping node robot local map generated gmapping package. map merging node multi-robot case, node merges local maps global map. package. Nodes types nodes; nodes detecting frontier points occupancy grid map, node filtering detected points, node assigning points robots. figure shows structure: global_rrt_frontier_detector global_rrt_frontier_detector node takes occupancy grid finds frontier points exploration targets) publishes detected points filter node process. multi-robot configuration, intended single instance node running. Running additional instances global frontier detector enhance speed frontier points detection, needed. Parameters ~map_topic (string, default: “/robot_1/map”): parameter defines topic node recieve map. ~eta (float, default: parameter controls growth rate RRT detection frontier points, unit meters. parameter set map size, large tree grow faster detect frontier points faster, large growth rate implies tree missing small corners map. Subscribed Topics map (Topic defined ~map_topic parameter) (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) clicked_point (geometry_msgs/PointStamped Message): global_rrt_frontier_detector node requires region explored defined. topic node recieves points define region. points defining square region explored, point tree starting point. publishing points topic, RRT start detecting frontier points. points intended published Rviz button. Simply add dynamic_reconfigure server component loading reconfigure service: import("rtt_dynamic_reconfigure"); loadService("my_component", "reconfigure") my_component.reconfigure.advertise("/my_component") disadvantage method minimum maximum values requrired dynamic_reconfigure unknown. current values properties advertised default values. overwrite automatically choosen minimum/maximum values calling advertise() operation: import("rtt_dynamic_reconfigure"); loadService("my_component", "reconfigure") my_component.reconfigure.min.int_property my_component.reconfigure.max.int_property my_component.reconfigure.advertise("~/my_component") custom config file rtt_dynamic_reconfigure package templated rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Server<ConfigType> class implements core functionality dynamic_reconfigure server. ConfigType class header generated automatically *.cfg file dynamic_reconfigure’s generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options() cmake macro. config file describes parameters minimum, maximum default values. order rtt_dynamic_reconfigure custom config, create .cfg file explained tutorial. add rtt_dynamic_reconfigure service plugin package: project(my_package) find_package(dynamic_reconfigure) generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options(cfg/MyPackage.cfg) orocos_plugin(my_package_reconfigure_service src/reconfigure_service.cpp) add_dependencies(rtt_dynamic_reconfigure_tests_service ${PROJECT_NAME}_gencfg) reconfigure_service.cpp source file instantiates rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Server<ConfigType> config type MyPackageConfig, implements rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::Updater<ConfigType> class explains fill config PropertyBag vice-versa registers server service plugin: #include <rtt_dynamic_reconfigure/server.h> #include <my_package/MyPackageConfig.h> header created generate_dynamic_reconfigure_options(cfg/MyPackage.cfg) namespace my_package; setProperty<double>("double_param", bag, config.double_param); setProperty<std::string>("str_param", bag, config.str_param); setProperty<bool>("bool_param", bag, config.bool_param); return true; static bool configFromProperties(MyPackageConfig &config, const RTT::PropertyBag &bag) getProperty<int>("int_param", bag, config.int_param); getProperty<double>("double_param", bag, config.double_param); getProperty<std::string>("str_param", bag, config.str_param); getProperty<bool>("bool_param", bag, config.bool_param); return true; namespace rtt_dynamic_reconfigure RTT_DYNAMIC_RECONFIGURE_SERVICE_PLUGIN(MyPackageConfig, "my_package_reconfigure") rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::setProperty<T>(...) rtt_dynamic_reconfigure::getProperty<T>(...) helper functions Updater implementation. SimClockActivityManager singleton class responsible coordinating execution SimClockActivity periodically (subject minimum desired periods). SimClockThread singleton thread responsible acutal updates RTT::os::TimeService optinally subscribing ROS messages /clock topic. default, SimClockThread running, started, override normal RTT TimeService update based update rate SimClockThread clock source. Usage SimClockActivity similarly RTT::PeriodicActivity. rtt_rosclock service plugin loaded, set number tasks’ activities SimClockActivities, set clock source, enable SimClockThread. ```cpp import(“rtt_ros”); ros.import(“rtt_rosclock”); create components my_component_1.setPeriod(0.01); my_component_2.setPeriod(1.0); Set simulation clock activities ros.clock.setSimClockActivity(my_component_1); File truncated lines full file

Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) rosservice.disconnectAll() Disconnects RTT operations operation callers ROS service servers clients. global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. description: This package provides an RTT service and service-requester for associating RTT component properties with ROS parameters

Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component “robot_description” ROS param File truncated lines full file

description: This package contains the components of the rtt_tf package
readme: RTT package Orocos RTT component utilizing rigid body transform library Orocos. component service operations requesting broadcasting sing batch transforms. Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

description:
ROS driver package for the thingmagic USB pro RFID reader.  The driver creates a node named /rfid_detector_node and 
publish all detected tags on the topic /RFID_detections as custom RFID_Detection messages.  The RFID_Detection messages have 4
fields: 
  epc:  A string containing the RFID tag identifier detected
  antenna:  8-bit integer containing the antenna number of the detection
  read_count:  8-bit integer containing the number of detections during the collection period
  rssi:  8-bit integer containing the RSSI value of the detection 

Uses the python wrapper for ThingMagic's Mercury API located here: https://github.com/gotthardp/python-mercuryapi
readme: ROS driver ThingMagic USB Pro RFID detector Install Package Dependencies (clone repo install): ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://gitlab.com/chambana/thingmagic_usbpro.git ./thingmagic_usbpro/install_script.sh ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Launch Package: roslaunch thingmagic_usbpro rfid_detector.launch Settings settings configured editing file thingmagic_usbpro/launch/rfid_detector.launch Device port default /dev/ttyACM0 Device Region code default North America Message Publish Rate (max) default Petr Gotthard maintaing Mercury API Python Wrapper

description: TinySLAM ROS implementation
readme: TinySLAM implementation ROS Description package ROS implentation tinySLAM (https://openslam.org/tinyslam.html) simpliest lightweight SLAM methods. Current implementation compatible ROS Jade adds improvements original method: alternative model grid map cell track average stored effective values (probability occupied scan’s quality account); option compute probability cell occupied based laser beam splits cell original implementation constant Hardware Requirements Current implementation supposes robot odometry data laser scan measurements horizontally mounted fixed laser scanner. data ROS topics Subscribed topics). launch-file run configure tinySLAM node: roslaunch tiny_slam tinyslam_run.launch path:=[path dataset] Dataset BAG format (http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag). comment rosbag node launch file real-time data replace dataset player. Nodes tiny_slam tiny_slam node takes sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages odometry data topic builds map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) retrieved ROS topic /map. Subscribed Topics (tf/tfMessage) Transforms relate frames laser, base, odometry laser_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scans create map Published Topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data topic (updated periodically) Parameters ~cell_type (string, default: “avg”) Accepted values: “avg” modified cell model; “base” original cell model ~occupancy_estimator (string, default: “const”) Accepted values: “const” original occupancy estimator; “area” dynamic occupancy estimator ~skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (boolean, default: false) Determines handle range laser measurements: true ignore measurements, false process empty cells. ~base_occupied_prob (float, default: initial probability cell occupied probability laser scan data valid) ~base_empty_prob (float, default: initial probability cell empty probability range laser scan data valid) Required Transforms base_link odom_combined: odometry system driver mobile base) Transforms map robot_pose: current estimate robot’s pose map frame Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

description: The adhoc_communication package allows to exchange data over an ad-hoc network setup by robots running the corresponding node. The package allows you to exchange data over serveral roscores by wrapping transmitting the data to the destination and publishing the data on a predefined topic at the destination host. Routing is accomplished using the hostname of the robots.

The explorer package utilizes frontier based exploration for multi-robot systems. Beside frontier detection, coordinated and uncoordinated exploration strategies are available to select goal points. Coordinated exploration enhances robot distribution and reduces redundancy in exploration reducing exploration time.

Detects persons through head and face detection and identifies the detected faces. The results are tracked over time to increase confidence. The system is optimized to be used with a Microsoft Kinect or Asus Xtion Pro Live sensor but could also handle different sensors as long as depth and color images are provided.

Contains several packages for detecting and identifying faces, and detecting and tracking humans with RGB-D sensors or laser scanners. 
		Detects persons through head and face detection and identifies the detected faces. The results are tracked over time to increase confidence. The system is optimized to be used with a Microsoft Kinect or Asus Xtion Pro Live sensor but could also handle different sensors as long as depth and color images are provided.

ROCON service application sending messages topic called requester. requester assigns universally unique identifier subscribes feedback topic hexadecimal string representation UUID, form /rocon_scheduler_0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef. scheduler provide status feedback topic scheduler_msgs/SchedulerRequests messages. Scheduler Resource Requests scheduler_msgs/SchedulerRequests message describes resources desired requester. status resource request passed requester scheduler scheduler_msgs/Request elements contained allocation feedback topics. Handling messages managing states resource request tricky, state flow topics simultaneously. schedulers requesters perform state transitions carefully consistently request. package interfaces schedulers requesters perform transitions correctly.

description: ROCON scheduler support packages.

This ROS package provides a simple, experimental, resource
    scheduler for the Robotics in Concert project.
readme: Overview systems require scheduling policies, Robotics Concert (ROCON) framework multiple scheduler implementations. scheduler ROS node running master ROCON Conductor, services Solution components. concert_simple_scheduler ROS package implements scheduler node Python. infrastructure packages scheduler implementations modify. ROS interface simple_scheduler node node simple fixed-priority scheduler allocates resources priority first-come, first-served basis. Subscribed topics rocon_scheduler (scheduler_msgs/SchedulerRequests) Scheduler requests. ROCON service application sending messages rocon_scheduler topic called requester. concert_client_changes (concert_msgs/ConcertClients) ROCON clients Conductor. Published topics rocon_scheduler_0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef (scheduler_msgs/SchedulerRequests) Per-requester scheduler feedback. requester assigns universally unique identifier subscribes feedback topic hexadecimal string representation UUID. resource_pool (scheduler_msgs/KnownResources) status ROCON clients managed scheduler. Parameters ~topic_name (string, default: rocon_scheduler) Common prefix request feedback topics. feedback topic generated dynamically, difficult remap. Usage rosrun concert_simple_scheduler simple_scheduler Protocol scheduler_msgs/SchedulerRequests message describes resources desired requester. status resource request passed requester scheduler scheduler_msgs/Request elements contained allocation feedback topics. Handling messages managing resource request states tricky, state flow topics simultaneously. schedulers requesters concert_scheduler_requests package perform state transitions request.

description: The industrial_desktop package contains all "released" packages
Metapackage for interaction cursor functionality.
A demo 3D cursor interaction application.

A message API for implementing a 6-DOF interaction cursor.
    A minimal implementation has one node that handles the user device I/O and
    another node (e.g. a plugin in Rviz) that uses the messages to interact with
    a marker.

An Rviz plugin that implements the interaction_cursor_msgs API
    to interact with interactive_markers.

readme: jsk_magnistartup SetUp (Running commands time robot) fix ROS_HOSTNAME robot Edit /etc/ubiquity/env.sh #!/bin/sh #export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname).local export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname) export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://$ROS_HOSTNAME:11311 Setup pigpid.services https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar, wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joan2937/pigpio/master/util/pigpiod.service sudo pigpiod.service /etc/systemd/system sudo systemctl enable pigpiod.service sudo systemctl start pigpiod.service add jsk_magni.service Add /etc/systemd/system/jsk-magni-startup.service file [Unit] Requires=roscore.service PartOf=roscore.service After=magni-base.service [Service] Type=simple User=ubuntu ExecStart=/usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target Add /usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup #!/bin/bash function log() logger user.$1 log info "magni-base: workspace setup file /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash" source /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash log_path="/tmp" source /etc/ubiquity/env.sh log info "magni-base: Launching ROS_HOSTNAME=$ROS_HOSTNAME, ROS_IP=$ROS_IP, ROS_MASTER_URI=$ROS_MASTER_URI, ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path" Punch export ROS_HOME=$(echo ~ubuntu)/.ros export ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path roslaunch jsk_magni_startup magni_bringup.launch PID=$! log info "jsk-magni-startup: Started roslaunch background process, PID $PID, ROS_LOG_DIR=$ROS_LOG_DIR" echo "$PID" $log_path/jsk-magni-startup.pid wait "$PID" Enable service sudo systemctl enable jsk-magni-startup.service sudo systemctl start jsk-magni-startup.service Add latest codes catkin_ws Update catkin_ws/src git: local-name: demos uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/demos.git version: 52136f04c0f39fe6a1001a17e208e5ce5b4cda61 git: local-name: magni_robot uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/magni_robot version: 603cae184bcc59b96d59f2fee6029b22725c3c5c git: local-name: pi_sonar uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar version: b84458f909d5febd410b8cd68d552e9aecad685c

Open O_NOATIME works UID completelly matchs caller priviledged (CAP_FOWNER) security reasons. launch mongodb shared database resouces, POSIX Capabilities Linux. Ubuntu sudo aptitude install libcap2-bin sudo setcap cap_fowner+ep /usr/bin/mongod Hark Microcone documentation Hark installation: http://www.hark.jp/wiki.cgi?page=HARK+Installation+Instructions hark jsk installation: https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty/blob/master/hark_jsk_plugins/INSTALL Microcone: http://www.hark.jp/wiki.cgi?page=SupportedHardware#p10 Bind rfcomm device binding rfcomm device, connect bluetooth device device file /dev/rfcomm1). rosserial bluetooth connection. detail, https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_robot/blob/master/jsk_robot_common/jsk_robot_startup/README.md#launchrfcomm_bindlaunch usage Save bluetooth device MAC address file /var/lib/robot/rfcomm_devices.yaml device1 address: XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX device2 address: YY:YY:YY:YY:YY:YY bind rfcomm devices. login root user ssh Assume bluetooth dongle plugged roslaunch jsk_pr2_startup pr2_rfcomm_bind.launch machine:=c2 check devices bound rfcomm, rfcomm command. ssh ssh rfcomm management pr2_rfcomm_bind.launch started automatically upstart. upstart config file /etc/upstart/jsk-rfcomm-bind.conf

libccd is library for collision detection between two convex shapes.
readme: libccd libccd library collision detection convex shapes. libccd implements variation Gilbert–Johnson–Keerthi algorithm Expand Polytope Algorithm (EPA) implements algorithm Minkowski Portal Refinement (MPR, a.k.a. XenoCollide) Game Programming Gems libccd open source library knowledge include MPR algorithm working space. library called mpr2d, implemented programming language, works space. libccd part ODE library ODE’s ./configure enable FCL library Willow Garage, Bullet3 library (https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3). implementation details GJK algorithm, http://www.win.tue.nl/~gino/solid/jgt98convex.pdf. Dependencies library based standard libraries. exception testsuites built top (https://github.com/danfis/cu) library licensed LGPL, testing depends libccd library distributed License libccd licensed OSI-approved 3-clause BSD License, text license distributed source code BSD-LICENSE file. file include license notice, rest considered licensed 3-clause BSD License. Compile Install libccd mechanisms compiling installing. simple Makefile, autotools, CMake. Makefile Directory src/ Makefile needed compilation installation: src/ install Library libccd default compiled double precision floating point numbers change options USE_SINGLE/USE_DOUBLE, USE_SINGLE=yes compile library single precision. Installation directory changed options PREFIX, INCLUDEDIR LIBDIR. info type Autotools libccd support autotools: Generate configure script ./bootstrap Create build/ directory: mkdir build build Run configure script: ../configure Run install: install configure script change libccd compiled installed, significant option –enable-double-precision enables double precision (single default case). r2_moveit_generated

A basic grasp generator for simple objects such as blocks or cylinders for use with the MoveIt! pick and place pipeline. 
    Does not consider friction cones or other dynamics.
readme: SUPPORT PACKAGE ENDED maintain. I’ll merge PRs happy share maintainership package interested. MoveIt! Simple Grasps basic grasp generator simple objects blocks cylinders MoveIt! pick place pipeline. friction cones dynamics. current implementation simple takes input pose vector (postition orientation) generates large number potential grasp approaches directions. includes grasp filter removing kinematically infeasible grasps threaded solvers. package includes: Simple pose-based grasp generator block Separate grasp generators custom objects rectanguar cylindrical objects Grasp filter Test code visualizations Developed Dave Coleman Correll Robotics Lab, University Colorado Boulder contributors. Video Demo simple demo Baxter: Build Status Install Ubuntu Debian Hydro: sudo apt-get install ros-hydro-moveit-simple-grasps Indigo: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-moveit-simple-grasps Install Source Clone repository catkin workspace, rosdep install tool automatically download dependencies. Depending current version ROS, Hydro: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro hydro Indigo: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Robot-Agnostic Configuration configuration file robot’s effector geometry. format repository config/baxter_grasp_data.yaml. comments file explanations. load file launch, copy file launch/grasp_test.launch line <rosparam command="load" file="$(find moveit_simple_grasps)/config/baxter_grasp_data.yaml"/>. Code Usage Note: find moveit_blocks.h discussed bottom page, helpful. discuss generation, filtering, visualization components. robot’s ROS package, add package package.xml, CMakeLists.txt. file add includes: Grasp generation visualization #include <moveit_simple_grasps/simple_grasps.h> #include <moveit_simple_grasps/grasp_data.h> #include <moveit_visual_tools/moveit_visual_tools.h> Add class’s member variables Grasp generator moveit_simple_grasps::SimpleGraspsPtr simple_grasps_; class publishing stuff rviz moveit_visual_tools::MoveItVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; robot-specific data generating grasps moveit_simple_grasps::GraspData grasp_data_; class’ constructor initialize visualization tools; Load Robot Tools publishing Rviz visual_tools_.reset(new moveit_visual_tools::MoveItVisualTools("base_link")); Change parameter visual tools robot’s base link. information package, moveit_visual_tools. load robot’s custom .yaml grasp data file: Load grasp data specific robot ros::NodeHandle File truncated lines full file

Sensors: /phantomx/joint_states Actuators (radians position control, arbitrary normalized speed cmd_vel): /phantomx/cmd_vel /phantomx/j_c1_lf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_c1_lm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_c1_lr_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_c1_rf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_c1_rm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_c1_rr_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_lf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_lm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_lr_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_rf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_rm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_thigh_rr_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_lf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_lm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_lr_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_rf_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_rm_position_controller/command /phantomx/j_tibia_rr_position_controller/command Python API Basic usage: import rospy phantomx_gazebo.phantomx import PhantomX rospy.init_node("walker_demo") phantomx=PhantomX() rospy.sleep(1) phantomx.set_walk_velocity(1,0,0) Set full speed secs rospy.sleep(5) phantomx.set_walk_velocity(0,0,0) Stop Dependencies ROS packages installed: gazebo_ros_control License software Génération Robots http://www.generationrobots.com HumaRobotics http://www.humarobotics.com Simplified BSD license

description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz
readme: Official ROS Documentation extensive standard ROS-style version documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof Install Configure ROS Package Install dependencies visualization): sudo apt-get install python-visual Ubuntu Ubuntu install https://github.com/lebarsfa/visual/tree/bionic, https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof/issues/47 sudo apt-get install python3-pip python3-wxgtk4.0 pip3 install vpython Ubuntu Download code: ~/catkin_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof.git catkin_make visualization, Ubuntu src/razor_imu_9dof/nodes wget https://www.glowscript.org/docs/VPythonDocs/VPtoGS.py python3 VPtoGS.py Converted/display_3D_visualization.py display_3D_visualization.py Install Arduino firmware SPX-15846 DEV-16832 (OpenLog Artemis), follow instructions OLA_IMU_Basics.ino sample https://github.com/sparkfun/OpenLog_Artemis drivers https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/how-to-install-ch340-drivers, install SparkFun Apollo3 boards Arduino IDE https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-board-definitions-in-the-arduino-ide (add https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sparkfun/Arduino_Boards/master/IDE_Board_Manager/package_sparkfun_index.json File Preferences Additional Board Manager URLs) ensure select SparkFun Apollo3 SparkFun RedBoard Artemis ATP board install SparkFun ICM IMU Arduino library https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-an-arduino-library). SEN-14001 (9DoF Razor IMU follow instructions default firmware https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/9dof-razor-imu-m0-hookup-guide updated version SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library https://github.com/lebarsfa/SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library updated version default firmware https://github.com/lebarsfa/9DOF_Razor_IMU). Open src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino Arduino IDE. Note: modified version Peter Bartz’ original Arduino code https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs). version emits linear acceleration angular velocity data required ROS Imu message Select hardware uncommenting line src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino, HARDWARE OPTIONS Select hardware uncommenting line! //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10125" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10736" (HMC5883L magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DoF Razor IMU version "SEN-14001" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "SPX-15846" #define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "DEV-16832" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10183" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10321" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10724" (HMC5883L magnetometer) Upload Arduino sketch board Configure default configuration, razor_imu_9dof expects yaml config file my_razor.yaml USB port Calibration parameters examplerazor.yaml file Copy file my_razor.yaml roscd razor_imu_9dof/config razor.yaml my_razor.yaml edit my_razor.yaml needed Launch Publisher roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub.launch Publisher visualization: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-and-display.launch Publisher diagnostics: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-diags.launch visualization roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-display.launch Calibrate File truncated lines full file

readme: s3000_laser Description Driver ROS read scan data device SICK driver based original driver developed Player/Stage Brian Gerkey, Kasper Stoy, Richard Vaughan, Andrew Howard. driver controls SICK safety laser scanner interpreting data output. driver basic assumes configured continuously output measured data data lines. Supported Hardware package work SICK safety laser scanners. Nodes laser driver. Published Topics: s3000_laser/scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Scan data laser, range data reflector detections. Parameters: ~port (string, default: "/dev/ttyUSB0") Serial port. ~frame_id (string, default: "/laser") Relative frame publication laser measures. ~baud_rate (int, default: Serial port baudrate (options: ~serial_parity (string, default: Serial port parity (options: "odd", ~serial_datasize (int, default: Character size mask serial link (options: ~range_min (float, default: Min valid range data produced. ~range_max (float, default: Max valid range data.

description:
High-level navigation application for the segbot allowing the segbot to
    approach and gothrough doors. The application can also be used to determine
    the segbot's logical location, as well as sense when a door in front of the
    robot is open or not.

Contains launch files for running the ROS navigation stack on the segbot
    using the eband_local_planner approach, as well as launch files for amcl
    and gmapping.

Contains sensor specific launch files and all the relevant filters that are
    applied on sensor data before being used by the segbot.
readme: Overview segbot_sensors package special-purpose drivers UTexas BWI segbots. ROS interfaces arduino_driver node reads serial messages Arduino board, converting data attached sensors ROS messages. Published topics battery0 (sensor_msgs/BatteryState) Main segbot battery voltage measurements. imu0 (sensor_msgs/Imu) Acceleration data inertial measurement unit. sonar (sensor_msgs/Range) Sonar ranges attached device frame. /diagnostics (diagnostic_msgs/DiagnosticArray) device diagnostics. Parameters ~baud (int, default: Baud rate Arduino serial port. ~port (string, default: /dev/ttyACM0) Serial port attached Arduino. Usage run driver node: rosrun segbot_sensors arduino_driver ranges_to_cloud node converts sonar Range messages point clouds. Subscribed topics sonar (sensor_msgs/Range) Sonar ranges attached device. Published topics sensor_ranges (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) centered distance point sonar range. Usage run arduino_driver node: roslaunch segbot_sensors arduino.launch

description: Stereo Slam
readme: Stereo SLAM stereo_slam [ROS][link_ros] node execute Simultaneous Localization Mapping (SLAM) stereo camera. algorithm designed tested underwater robotics. node based [G2O][link_g2o] library graph optimization power [libhaloc][link_libhaloc] find loop closures graph nodes. keyframe multi-keyframe loop closing mechanism, based keypoint clustering, improve SLAM corrections feature-poor environments. action Specially designed underwater scenarios: [Video: ORB-SLAM Stereo SLAM (underwater)][link_yt_3] videos… [Video: Stereo SLAM reconstruction ][link_yt_1] [Video: Stereo SLAM UIB outdoor pond][link_yt_2] Installation (Ubuntu ROS Indigo) Install dependencies sudo apt-get install ros-<your ros distro>-libg2o sudo apt-get install libceres-dev setup stereo visual odometer [viso2][link_viso2] [fovis][link_fovis]). Download code, save ROS workspace enviroment compile. roscd git clone https://github.com/srv/stereo_slam.git stereo_slam rosmake Parameters odom_topic Visual odometry topic (type nav_msgs::Odometry). camera_topic namespace stereo camera. (hard-coded) parameters include/constants.h set node parameters. Default parameters work. Run node run node launch file performance scale images width). <launch> <arg name="camera" default="/stereo"/> Run stereo image proc <node ns="$(arg camera)" pkg="stereo_image_proc" type="stereo_image_proc" name="stereo_image_proc" <node pkg="viso2_ros" type="stereo_odometer" name="stereo_odometer"> <remap from="stereo" to="$(arg camera)"/> <remap from="image" to="image_rect"/> </node> <node pkg="stereo_slam" type="localization" name="stereo_slam" output="screen"> <param name="odom_topic" value="/stereo_odometer/odometry"/> <param name="camera_topic" value="$(arg camera)"/> </node> </launch> Published Topics /stereo_slam/odometry vehicle pose (type nav_msgs::Odometry). /stereo_slam/graph_poses updated graph poses (type stereo_slam::GraphPoses). /stereo_slam/keyframes Number inserted keyframes (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/keypoints_clustering Image keypoint clusters (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings Image loop closing correspondences. Correspondences keyframe-to-multi-keyframe (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_queue Number keyframes waiting loop closing queue. monitor topic, check real-time performance: number grows indefinitely means system able process keyframes, scale images. (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/loop_closings Number loop closings (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/pointcloud pointcloud keyframe (type sensor_msgs::PointCloud2). /stereo_slam/tracking_overlap Image representation traking overlap. decide insert keyframe graph (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/camera_params optimized (calibrated) camera parameters loop closure (type stereo_slam::CameraParams). Saved data node stores data stereo_slam directory execution: haloc folder files needed libhaloc library, responsible loop closing detection. folder keyframes Stores left stereo image keyframe possibility drawing keypoint clustering image). loop_closures Stores images published topic /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings. pointclouds Stores pointclouds published topic /stereo_slam/pointcloud. description: Dynamixel and 3mxl driver

This library offers an introspection mechanism for C/C++ value-types. I.e.
      it offers a way to represent types, and to manipulate in-memory values
      that are instances of those types.

      A Ruby binding is included, which gives a fast and transparent
      modification of C/C++ in-memory types from Ruby.

description:
This is a driver providing data from VICON motion capture systems. It is based on the vicon_mocap package from the starmac stacks. 
     Additionally, it can handle multiple subjects / segments and allows to calibrate an origin of the vehicle(s) as this is somehow tedious with the VICON Tracker.
readme: driver providing data VICON motion capture systems. based vicon_mocap package starmac stacks. Additionally, handle multiple subjects segments calibrate origin vehicle(s) tedious VICON Tracker. License: BSD Author: Markus Achtelik <markus.achtelik@mavt.ethz.ch> QUICK START roslaunch vicon_bridge vicon.launch set "datastream_hostport" parameter vicon computer's ip/hostname OPERATION vicon_bridge node initiates connection Vicon data source Nexus Tracker) DataStream API. parameter ~datastream_hostport set address port (joined colon) DataStream server machine, default port). parameter ~stream_mode call DataStream SetStreamMode method; valid values "ServerPush", "ClientPull". subjects segments recognized automatically published transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped ORIGIN CALIBRATION object created vicon tracker, places origin arbitrary object. Setting desired origin Tracker tedious, added origin calibration method. Place vehicle vicon origin set vicon calibration, measure/estimate height offset) desired origin floor. run rosrun vicon_bridge calibrate <subject <segment offset> Poses grabbed, averaged vehicle vicon's origin. calibration parameters stored parameter server ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose startup, node checks calibration parameters time running rviz observe correct Published topics: vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> publishes subjects/segments vicon/markers publishes labeled unlabeled markers. Labeled markers affected origin calibration Services: grab_vicon_pose grab poses subject/segment averages calibrate_segment calls origin calibration segment transforms vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> Parameters stream_mode mode connect DataStream server. Values: "ClientPull", "ServerPush". Default: "ClientPull" datastream_hostport host:port DataStream server tf_ref_frame_id reference frame Default: "world" ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/w ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/x ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/y ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/z quaternion pose origin vehicle ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/x ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/y ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/z position pose origin vehicle

The camera_handler package

The contact_handler package. It reports the dynamic-engine-reported contacts between
      all scene objects on a topic called "contacts".

      This package uses gazebo_msgs/ContactsState message type to report body contacts.
      This is because ROS doesn't have any standardized contact reporting messages.
      However, to have gazebo_msgs, you don't need to install whole Gazebo, you just need to
      install the single package ros-indigo-gazebo-msgs.

description: Plugin for nose or py.test that automatically reruns flaky tests.
readme: flaky Flaky plugin nose py.test automatically reruns flaky tests. Ideally, tests reliably pass fail, test fixtures rely components reliable. flaky, removing tests marking \@skip, automatically retried. information flaky, presentation. Marking tests flaky mark test flaky, simply import flaky decorate test \@flaky: flaky import flaky @flaky def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') default, flaky retry failing test behavior overridden passing values flaky decorator. accepts parameters: max_runs, min_passes; flaky run tests max_runs times, succeeded min_passes times. test passes min_passes times, considered success; run max_runs times passing min_passes times, considered failure. @flaky(max_runs=3, min_passes=2) def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): test pass run times.""" value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') Marking class flaky addition marking single test flaky, entire test cases marked flaky: @flaky class TestMultipliers(TestCase): def test_flaky_doubler(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') @flaky(max_runs=3) def test_flaky_tripler(self): value_to_triple result get_result_from_flaky_tripler(value_to_triple) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_triple 'Result tripled incorrectly.') \@flaky class decorator mark test_flaky_doubler flaky, override max_runs test_flaky_tripler decorator test method). rerun types failures Depending tests, failures due flakiness. rerunning failures, filter function flaky fail test def is_not_crash(err, *args): return issubclass(err[0], ProductCrashedError) @flaky def test_something(): raise ProductCrashedError @flaky(rerun_filter=is_not_crash) def test_something_else(): raise ProductCrashedError File truncated lines full file

Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands for "generate tests". It promotes generative testing, where a single test can execute over a variety of input.
readme: genty Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands "generate tests". promotes generative testing, single test execute variety input. Genty breeze. file sample.py code test tests: genty import genty, genty_repeat, genty_dataset unittest import TestCase class test class MyClass(object): def add_one(self, return test code @genty class MyClassTests(TestCase): @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) Running MyClassTests default unittest runner python unittest sample test_add_one(0, (sample.MyClassTests) test_add_one(100000, (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests write multiple independent tests test cases, code refactored parametrized genty! produces readable tests. produces maintainable tests. produces expressive tests. option running test multiple times. stress tests exercising code race conditions. test @genty_repeat(3) def test_adding_one_to_zero(self): self.assertEqual(1, MyClass().add_one(0)) run times, producing output python unittest sample test_adding_one() iteration_1 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_2 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_3 (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests techniques combined: @genty_repeat(2) @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) options explore including naming datasets genty_args. python File truncated lines full file

description: Grasp Synergies (aka eigengrasps): compute, store, and command low-dimensional grasp representations.
readme: Grasp Synergy ROS package package implements grasp synergies (aka eigengrasps): low-dimensional representation hand configurations. basically, grasp synergies computed PCA bunch hand configurations (joint angles) infer low-dimensional representation. Points low-dimensional space reprojected full configuration space control hand. ROS package independent hand configuration. consists components: GraspSynergy class: compute store synergies high-dimensional grasp configurations, compute grasps low-dimensional input. synergy_node: ROS magic provide nice interfaces controlling grasp synergies. grasp_synergy Grasp Synergies (aka eigengrasps) low-dimensional representations grasp learned PCA. GraspSynergy class convenience class computing, storing, commanding grasp synergies. hand-agnostic. Grasping data form: numpy matrix joint values list sensor_msgs/JointState messages filepath rosbag file. number collected grasps full dimensionality hand. extract principal components (synergies), practice smaller number. compute grasp original space), simply pass B-dimensional vector coefficients compute_grasp. (Nominally, method automatically figure components return grasp configuration original D-dimensional space. synergy_node synergy node enables load grasp synergy space, create subscribers synergy space, publish desired joint state messages control hand. creates top-level subscriber fully-specified coefficient vector per-component subscribers component single time node receives low-dimensional (synergy-space) point, computes hand configuration publishes message desired joint states. Subscribers: node create variable number subscribers: fully-specified coefficient vector, component singleton values). default, node trained bag file hand configuration data. subscribers synergies: /grasp_synergy [std_msgs/Float32MultiArray] /grasp_synergy/syn_0 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_1 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_2 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_3 [std_msgs/Float32] enables tools rqt_ez_publisher (http://wiki.ros.org/rqt_ez_publisher) provide nice slider GUI interface synergy space: NOTE: Due small bug rqt_ez_publisher, sliders control individual synergies (/grasp_synergy/syn_N) latest rqt_ez_publisher (checkout github workspace run local copy). description: The android_speech_pkg package
map_manager
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Raphael Memmesheimer, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer
readme: map_manager Issues Todo’s Aus bisher ungeklärten Gründen kann seltenen Fällen passieren, dass der map_manager die Verbindung zum roscore verliert. diesem Fall muss durch rosrun map_manager map_manager neugestartet werden. Introduction Der map_manager ist der Mittelpunkt der Kommunikation zwischen homer_mapping, homer_navigation, GUI und die Spiel-Nodes. Das Zusammenspiel dieser Nodes ist Screenshot des rqt_graphs sehen. verwaltet die aktuell durch das mapping erstellte Karte sowie weitere Kartebenen. Aktuell sind das die SLAM-Karte, die aktuellen Laserdaten einer weiteren Ebene und eine Masking-Ebene, der mit Hilfe der GUI Hindernisse oder freie Flächen die Karte gezeichnet werden können. Jedes mal, wenn eine SLAM-Karte von der mapping-Node geschickt wird, wird diese mit allen anderen Karteneben überschrieben der Reihenfolge SLAM, Masking, Laserdaten) und als eine zusammengefügte Karte versendet. Zudem verwaltet der map_manager alle erstellten Points Interest (POIs), die als Ziele die Navigation verwendet werden. Die Node ist außerdem zuständig das Speichern und Laden der Kartenebenen und der POIs. Dabei wird die SLAM-Ebene sowie die Masking-Ebene berücksichtigt. Topics Publisher /map: Die aktuelle Karte, die aus allen aktivierten Kartenebenen zusammengesetzt ist. Diese wird der GUI angezeigt und die Navigation verwendet. /map_manager/poi_list: Verschickt einen Vektor mit allen aktuellen POIs. Dieser Publisher wird immer ausgelöst, sobald sich ein POI ändert oder ein neuer hinzugefügt wird. /map_manager/loaded_map: Wenn eine Karte geladen wird, wird über dieses Topic die geladene SLAM-Ebene die homer_mapping-Node verschickt. /map_manager/mask_slam: Über die GUI kann die SLAM-Map verändert werden. Diese Modifizierungen werden über dieses Topic vom map_manager das homer_mapping versendet. Subscriber /homer_mapping/slam_map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): Hierüber wird die aktuelle SLAM-Map empfangen. /map_manager/save_map (map_messages/SaveMap): Hierüber wird der Befehl zum Speichern der Karte inklusive des Dateinamens empfangen. /map_manager/load_map (map_messages/SaveMap): Hiermit wird eine Karte geladen und alle bisherigen Kartenebenen durch die geladenen ersetzt. /map_manager/toggle_map_visibility (map_messages/MapLayers): Hierüber können einzelne Kartenebenen aktiviert beziehungsweise deaktiviert werden. Deaktivierte werden nicht mehr beim Zusammenfügen der Karte berücksichtigt und dementsprechend auch nicht der GUI angezeigt sowie die Navigation verwendet. /scan (nav_msgs/LaserScan): Der aktuelle Laserscan, der die Laserscan-Ebene gezeichnet wird. /map_manager/add_POI (map_messages/PointOfInterest): Hierüber kann ein POI hinzugefügt werden. /map_manager/modify_POI (map_messages/ModifyPOI): Hierüber kann ein vorhandener POI verändert werden Position,…) /map_manager/delete_POI (map_messages/DeletePointOfInterest): Hierüber kann ein vorhander POI gelöscht werden. /map_manager/modify_map (map_messages/ModifyMap): Über dieses Topic werden die Koordinaten der Polygone verschickt, die über die GUI maskiert wurden. Außerdem wird die Kartenebene mitgeteilt, die verändet werden soll (SLAM oder Masking-Ebene). /map_manager/reset_maps (std_msgs/Empty): Hierüber werden alle Kartenebenen zurückgesetzt.
